content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the failure reconciliation implementation and its daemon/controller integration. I found 2 fix-now correctness issues: one undercounts failures when same-run retries reopen lineage after a missing journal write, and one can bind failure reconciliation to a stale snapshot attempt when `workflow_run_id` is absent.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: Same-run-id retries can permanently lose an earlier failed attempt and undercount toward `MAX_FAILURE_RETRIES`. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:167-193`, the stale-replay branch returns immediately whenever a newer attempt reused the same `run_id`. Because `src/adapters/fs.rs:4536-4583` reopens that failed attempt in place instead of preserving a separate lineage row, a partial write where the older `BeadFailed` journal append was the missing piece leaves no durable record of that earlier failure anywhere. The next failure is then counted as attempt 1 again, so escalation to `NeedsOperator` can be delayed past the configured retry limit. affected_files: [`src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs`, `src/adapters/fs.rs`]. remediation_hint: when a replay is still identifiable by `(run_id, started_at)`, repair or append the missing `BeadFailed` journal row without mutating the newer running attempt, so historical failures remain countable even when same-run retries reopen lineage in place.
   Rationale: This breaks the core retry-budget guarantee in crash-recovery / partial-write scenarios, which is one of the main responsibilities of this bead.
2. finding_summary: Snapshot fallback can reconcile the wrong run when `workflow_run_id` is missing. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1998-2015`, `try_reconcile_failure()` accepts any `active_run`/`interrupted_run` from the run snapshot as the failed attempt when the daemon task lacks a durable run binding; `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2018-2051` then derives `failed_at` and the error summary for that run. If the snapshot is stale but the project journal already contains a newer durable `run_started`/`run_resumed` for a different run, the daemon will mark the older attempt failed and may block or escalate the bead based on the wrong execution. affected_files: [`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs`]. remediation_hint: only use snapshot fallback when it matches the newest durable journal/lineage attempt; otherwise fail closed to `NeedsOperator`, consistent with the exact-binding checks already enforced for persisted `workflow_run_id`.
   Rationale: This is a misattribution bug in the reconciliation path: it can mutate milestone state for the wrong run even though this change is specifically trying to make failed-attempt reconciliation exact and durable.
